label: Film / series / book review
opening_rule: First sentence: your verdict -- not what it is, not who made it, not the plot. Is it worth your time or not, and why in one specific claim. Max 20 words. Must work as a standalone opinion.
closing_rule: Final paragraph: name the specific audience that will love this and the specific audience that will bounce off it. No 'if you enjoy X you might like this'. Be direct. End on the one thing that will stick with you in six months -- or won't.

sections.name: The verdict upfront
sections.purpose: Your overall assessment in 2-3 sentences. Does it deliver what it promises? Is the hype justified? Take a position immediately -- do not build to it.

sections.name: What it actually is
sections.purpose: One paragraph. Genre, format, runtime, premise -- factual, no spin. Distinguish what the marketing says from what the thing actually is. If it was sold as one thing and is another, say so.

sections.name: What works
sections.purpose: Name specific scenes, characters, writing decisions, technical choices that succeed. Not 'the acting is great' -- name who, name the scene, name why it works. Minimum two specific examples.

sections.name: What does not work
sections.purpose: Name specific failures -- pacing, plot holes, tonal inconsistency, missed opportunities. Every release has them. If this one genuinely has none, explain why that itself is unusual. Do not sanitize criticism.

sections.name: Why it matters or does not
sections.purpose: Does this change anything in its genre or medium? Is it a landmark or a competent entry? Compare to one or two specific predecessors -- not to 'similar films in the genre'. If the industry or cultural context makes this release significant beyond its quality, explain that specifically.
